project: OpenTabs Platform — Slack Tab Detection & Side Panel State
description: Fix two bugs preventing the Slack plugin from working: (1) auth extraction fails on modern app.slack.com because boot_data/TS globals are no longer populated, and (2) the side panel displays stale 'closed' tab state due to a race condition between config.getState and tab.syncAll.

userStories.title: Fix Slack auth extraction for modern app.slack.com client
userStories.description: As a user with app.slack.com open in my browser, I want the Slack plugin to detect my authenticated session so that isReady() returns true and all Slack tools work.
acceptanceCriteria: isSlackAuthenticated() returns true when called in the context of an authenticated app.slack.com tab
getAuth() successfully extracts the xoxc- session token from the page
slackApi() can make successful API calls using the extracted token
The Slack plugin reports 'ready' tab state (not 'unavailable') when the user is logged into app.slack.com
The existing getAuthFromLocalStorage() path still works for old WORKSPACE.slack.com URLs
The existing getAuthFromBootData() path still works if boot_data happens to be present
cd plugins/slack && bun run build succeeds (generates updated adapter.iife.js and opentabs-plugin.json)
userStories.notes:
## Problem

The Slack plugin's `isReady()` calls `isSlackAuthenticated()` which calls `getAuth()` in `plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts:108`. This tries two sources:
1. `getAuthFromLocalStorage()` (lines 35-62): Reads `localConfig_v2`/`localConfig_v3` from localStorage — only works on old `WORKSPACE.slack.com` URLs
2. `getAuthFromBootData()` (lines 70-98): Reads `window.boot_data.api_token` or `window.TS.boot_data.api_token` — the modern `app.slack.com` client no longer reliably populates these globals

The MCP server health endpoint (`curl localhost:9515/health`) reports `tabState: 'unavailable'` for Slack, confirming the tab IS found but `isReady()` returns false.

## Investigation needed

The modern Slack web client at `app.slack.com` stores auth tokens differently. Common locations to check (open DevTools on a live app.slack.com page):

1. **Redux/MobX store**: Slack may store the token in an internal state store accessible via React DevTools or `document.querySelector('[data-reactroot]').__reactFiber$`
2. **Inline `<script>` tags**: The initial HTML may contain a `<script>` tag with JSON config that includes `api_token`. Search `document.querySelectorAll('script')` for content containing `xoxc-` or `api_token`.
3. **localStorage with different keys**: The key names may have changed from `localConfig_v2`/`v3`. Enumerate all localStorage keys and search for token-like values.
4. **Performance entries / XHR interception**: The token may appear in API call headers. Check `performance.getEntriesByType('resource')` or intercept fetch/XHR.
5. **`window.__NEXT_DATA__`** or similar SSR hydration data: Some Slack client builds use Next.js.
6. **Cookie-based auth**: The `d` cookie on `.slack.com` is the session cookie. Combined with a CSRF token from the page, this may be sufficient.

## Implementation approach

Add a new function `getAuthFromPageScripts()` (or similar) that extracts the token from wherever the modern client stores it. Chain it into the existing `getAuth()` function:

```typescript
const getAuth = (): SlackAuth | null => 
  getAuthFromLocalStorage() ?? getAuthFromBootData() ?? getAuthFromPageScripts();
```

The new function should:
- Search inline `<script>` tags for `api_token` patterns
- Check all localStorage keys (not just `localConfig_v2`/`v3`)
- Check `document.cookie` for the `d` cookie and look for CSRF tokens in the DOM
- Return `SlackAuth` with token, workspaceUrl, and teamId

File to edit: `plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts`

## After making changes

Rebuild the plugin: `cd plugins/slack && bun run build`
This generates `plugins/slack/dist/adapter.iife.js` which the MCP server's file watcher picks up automatically.

## IMPORTANT: Extension reload

Plugin adapter changes are picked up by the MCP server's file watcher — no extension reload needed. The file watcher detects changes to `dist/adapter.iife.js`, reads the new content, and sends a `plugin.update` notification to the extension which re-injects the adapter into matching tabs.

However, if you change any code in `platform/browser-extension/src/`, you MUST:
1. Run `bun run build` (from repo root)
2. Tell the user to reload the extension from chrome://extensions/ (click the refresh icon on the OpenTabs card)
3. Tell the user to close and reopen the side panel

userStories.title: Fix side panel stale tab state display
userStories.description: As a user viewing the OpenTabs side panel, I want to see the correct tab state for each plugin so that I know whether my Slack tab is detected, needs login, or is ready.
acceptanceCriteria: When the side panel opens while connected, it displays the correct tab state from the server (not stale 'closed')
tab.stateChanged notifications that arrive while fetchConfigState is in-flight are not lost
When WebSocket reconnects (sp:connectionState connected=true), the side panel updates to the correct state after the reconnection handshake completes
If the server has 'unavailable' in tabMapping when config.getState is called, the side panel shows 'Log in to Slack' (amber dot), not 'Open slack.com' (red dot)
The 200ms debounce in loadPlugins does not cause tab state updates to be dropped
